title: Migrating from fabric-cicd to fabio deploy
summary: Guide for teams migrating from Microsoft's fabric-cicd Python library to fabio's deploy commands. Shows the equivalent config mappings, parameter format translation, and additional capabilities available in fabio.

parameter_mapping.note: The find_replace format is identical between fabric-cicd and fabio. Just rename the file from parameter.yml to parameters.json and wrap in JSON syntax (or keep YAML — fabio supports both).

additional_capabilities: fabio deploy plan: preview changes before applying (fabric-cicd has no plan mode)
fabio deploy validate: offline pre-flight checks (no API calls needed)
fabio deploy export: export from live workspace to disk (fabric-cicd requires Git)
fabio deploy init-params: auto-scaffold parameter file by scanning definitions
Content-hash diffing: only updates items that actually changed (fabric-cicd always redeploys all)
Rename detection: preserves item IDs when items are renamed
Post-deploy hooks: semantic model refresh, environment publish, value set activation, schedule creation
--delete-orphans: remove items not in source (with protected type safety)
--git-diff: only deploy items changed since a git ref
migration_steps: 2. Translate deploy.yml → deploy-config.yaml using the mapping above
3. Translate parameter.yml → parameters.json (format is identical, just rename/convert)
4. Test with: fabio deploy plan --config deploy-config.yaml --env test
5. Apply with: fabio deploy apply --config deploy-config.yaml --env test
6. Verify convergence: fabio deploy plan --config deploy-config.yaml --env test (should show all skip)
agent_guidance: When a user has an existing fabric-cicd setup (deploy.yml + parameter.yml), help them translate to fabio's config format. The parameter find_replace format is identical — only the config file structure differs. Fabio's --config file supports both JSON and YAML.
